sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, a 45-year-old male with a history of diabetes type two and mild intermittent asthma, presents with a 3-week history of right elbow pain, exacerbated by playing tennis and activities that involve repetitive stress.
The pain is described as sharp, stabbing, and throbbing, rated an 8/10 with activity and 3/10 at rest.
The patient reports trying ibuprofen and Tylenol without significant relief.
He denies recent trauma, fever, or other symptoms.
He also mentions that his blood sugars have been high, ranging from 150-160 mg/dL, and that he has been consuming excessive candy.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes type two, mild intermittent asthma, a history of gallbladder removal 10 years ago, and an allergy to penicillin.
Review of systems: The patient denies fever, but reports high blood sugars.
Current medications: Metformin 500mg twice a day, Flovent twice a day, Albuterol as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has a moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow, mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist, and a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The cardiovascular exam is otherwise normal, and there is no palpable joint effusion.
The right elbow x-ray shows no acute fracture or other bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow, also known as tennis elbow, likely due to overuse from increased tennis activity.
Problem list: Acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow; diabetes type two; mild acute intermittent asthma.
Plan:
I prescribed Ultram 50mg once a day for pain management.
I recommend resting the right arm as much as possible, avoiding repetitive stress, and icing the right elbow for 20 minutes, 3 times a day.
I will provide a brace for the right forearm to help with discomfort.
I increased the patient's metformin to 1000mg twice a day to manage his diabetes.
I ordered a hemoglobin A1c level, CBC, and chem-12 blood test to monitor his diabetes control.
I will also provide a refill for Albuterol.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in 4 weeks to reevaluate the right elbow and adjust treatment as needed.
